channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Banco Falabella Perú: los ataques silenciosos desafían a la banca
item.description: Un intruso puede permanecer durante meses dentro de la infraestructura de una entidad financiera sin ser detectado, una tendencia donde los atacantes están apostando por accesos silenciosos para explorar sistemas y escalar privilegios antes de ejecutar un ataque, advierten desde Banco Falabella Perú. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: AI’s Impact: Tech and Finance Sectors Losing 28,000 Jobs Monthly
item.description: The weakness stands out against an otherwise robust labor market that created more than 113,000 jobs monthly this year through May — a number that would have been much higher if the banking and tech industries hadn’t dragged down the overall total. Related: OpenAI’s Altman Says AI Unlikely to Lead to ‘Jobs Apocalypse’ After investing heavily in artificial intelligence, tech companies are now increasingly citing AI as a factor for cutting their workforce. A decline in payrolls in the financial-activities and information sectors — where AI adoption rates have been fastest — has accelerated in 2026, to 28,000 per month on average based on government data.

item.title: Pickups Dominate Top 20 Vehicles Sold in US in The Second Quarter
item.description: Pickup trucks remained among the top vehicles sold in the U.S., but a few sedans including the Honda Accord were popular purchases in the second quarter, a new list shows. Reuters compiled a list the 20 top-selling vehicles in the United States in Q2 2026, as reported by automakers and ranked by total units. Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: Willis appoints Gary McMullen as senior director for workplace risk
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aviso Specialty hires Kerin Rathgeber as trade credit head
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Niyam Group taps Ritesh Shah to drive India engineering, construction buildout
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA publishes landmark review into impact of AI on retail financial services
item.description: The review sets out how AI could reshape retail financial services for consumers, firms, markets and regulators by 2030 and beyond. Drawing on views from across the financial services landscape, the report identifies 4 major AI‑driven shifts likely to impact retail financial services: the transformation of firm operations; the evolution of consumer journeys; the reshaping of competition and market power; and the amplification of fraud and cyber risks. The Review concludes that AI is likely to become a defining force in retail financial services, transforming how firms operate, how consumers make financial decisions and how markets function.

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending July 5th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending July 5th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending July 5th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Cat bond fund UCITS average 0.62% return in June. 12-month rolling-return 10.22%
item.description: According to the latest data from the Plenum CAT Bond UCITS Fund Indices , catastrophe bond fund strategies in the UCITS format averaged a 0.62% return for the month of June 2026 which beat last year, while the rolling-twelve month return rose slightly to 10.22% as a result of the stronger month. As a result, the average year-to-date performance of the UCITS format catastrophe bond fund strategies rose from 2.18% at May 29th to now 2.91% as of June 26th 2026, the latest data available and closest we have to a half-year return for these catastrophe bond funds. You can analyse the Plenum CAT Bond UCITS Fund Indices in our charts: Interestingly, for June the performance advantage switched to the lower-risk cohort of cat bond funds in the UCITS format, with these averaging a 0.66% return for the month, while the higher-risk cat bond funds averaged 0.60%.

item.title: AXA XL’s new catastrophe bond is the first ever to cover US terrorism risk
item.description: As a result, this Galileo Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond is the first ever to provide a sponsor with protection against terrorism events in the United States. We’ve now learned this deal was particularly innovative and set some new market firsts, including becoming the first US terrorism cat bond ever. Most prominent are the four Baltic PCC Limited catastrophe bonds issued for the UK terrorism reinsurer Pool Re , as well as the Athéna I Reinsurance DAC cat bond for the French terrorism risk pool Gareat.

item.title: Klapton Re welcomes Mukuma Musenge as Chief Technology Officer
item.description: Zambia-based reinsurer Klapton Reinsurance has announced the appointment of Mukuma Musenge as Chief Technology Officer. His career also includes a three-year tenure at Kuala Tech as Chief Executive Officer, as well as close to two years as Information Technology Manager at Klapton Re. In his new position, Musenge will lead Klapton Re’s technology function, focusing on driving the development of integrated systems and data-led solutions that improve efficiency, enhance customer experience, and support smarter business decision-making.

item.title: Echo Re gets IFS rating upgrade to A+ by Fitch
item.description: Fitch Ratings has upgraded the Insurer Financial Strength (IFS) rating of Echo Reinsurance Limited (Echo Re), a Swiss-based reinsurer, from A to A+, with a stable outlook. The upgrade reflects Echo Re’s strengthened strategic importance within its parent, DEVK Group, supported by sustained financial performance, increasing geographical diversification, and “growing synergies” with its sister company, DEVK Re, says Fitch. The rating continues to reflect Echo Re’s ownership by DEVK, while DEVK Re was also revised up from ‘bbb+’, driven by the company’s reduced business risk and notable geographical diversification, “very strong capitalisation,” and structural improvements in financial performance.

item.title: Athene appoints Larik Hall as SVP and Head of Japan
item.description: Athene, a retirement solutions company and subsidiary of Apollo Global Management, Inc., has announced the appointment of Larik Hall as Senior Vice President and Head of Japan. “Larik’s strong track record of building retirement businesses and meaningful experience leading large-scale insurance platforms in the Japanese market makes him exceptionally well suited to expand Athene’s footprint locally.” “I am honoured to join Athene and lead its Japan strategy at such an important time,” added Hall. Based in Japan, Hall will lead Athene’s growth strategy to accelerate the expansion of retirement solutions in the country.

item.title: Alabama DOI Lifts 16-Month Moratorium on New Captive Insurers in the State
item.description: Now that a new Alabama law has had time to take effect, the state Department of Insurance has lifted a 16-month moratorium on new captive insurance companies in the state. A captive manager and supporter of the new law said the move is good news for Alabama and for firms who want to domicile captives in the state. “The lifting of this moratorium has occurred at an ideal time as Alabama is continuously explored as a strong option for filers seeking new risk management strategies.” Alabama Insurance Commissioner Mark Fowler did not say why he imposed the temporary moratorium on processing new captives in March 2025, then extended it last October.

item.title: FBI Probes Whether Alarum Unit Is Behind Co-Opted Home Devices
item.description: The FBI is investigating whether a subsidiary of the data-collection firm Alarum Technologies Ltd. had a role in linking customers’ home internet devices without their consent into a network that people can use to disguise their locations, according to documents seen by Bloomberg News and confirmed by people familiar with the situation. For more than a year, US Federal Bureau of Investigation agents have been examining potential links between NetNut, the Israel-based Alarum subsidiary that sells access to such networks, and software known as Popa that’s allegedly used to co-opt people’s devices, according to records and people familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified discussing an active investigation.

item.title: Tesco and Aviva launch free life coverage for new parents
item.description: Tesco Insurance and Money Services has launched Free Parent Life Cover in partnership with Aviva , offering eligible parents £15,000 of free life insurance for 12 months for each child under the age of four. Alongside the free coverage, Tesco Insurance and Money Services will provide educational content and tools to help parents assess their protection needs, as well as personalized communications, Clubcard offers, and partner benefits aimed at supporting early parenthood. Bottom Line: Tesco is following a playbook already used by financial apps such as Acorns, Stash, UNest, and Payfare, as well as AXA Philippines, by offering free life insurance to deepen customer engagement and introduce consumers to protection products.

item.title: Not blowout year, but M&A resiliency remains amid soft market, improving geopolitical outlook
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Starwind Specialty launches high-value homes program
item.description: Starwind Specialty has launched Starwind High Value Homes, a new underwriting management program for residential properties with insured values of $5 million and above. According to the company, the program was developed in response to volatility in the high-value homeowners market, reduced capacity for complex residential risks, and growing demand for more flexible coverage options. The program will be distributed through CRC Specialty’s wholesale brokers, targeting the high-net-worth personal lines market.

item.title: Ardonagh launches specialty insurtech Axiiem
item.description: The Ardonagh Group has launched Axiiem, a specialty insurance technology business aimed at connecting brokers, insurers, and capacity providers through a proprietary digital trading platform. Ardonagh said Axiiem uses technology developed internally over several years to streamline specialty insurance placement through data-driven underwriting and portfolio distribution. The launch follows Ardonagh’s investments in data infrastructure, analytics, and AI, including the creation of Ardonagh Intelligence in 2025.

item.title: Marsh appoints Melody Carr as national head of technical claims advocacy for the Pacific
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: June 2026 Agency Appointment Report
item.description: Coverager published its Agency Distribution report for June 2026. Notable moves on the P&C side: CoverTree, the manufactured home insurance agency that has raised $25 million to date, was appointed by Integon, part of Allstate. NEXT Insurance appointed 29 agencies, including established commercial brokerages like Hibbs-Hallmark & Company and a less obvious fit — Buy Life Insurance Now, LLC, a life insurance agency.

item.title: Prudential seeks $135 million from R&W insurers over Assurance IQ deal
item.description: Prudential Financial is seeking at least $135 million from a group of insurers that provided representations and warranties (R&W) insurance for its $2.35 billion acquisition of Assurance IQ , arguing the carriers have spent more than five years avoiding a coverage decision after the deal unraveled. “Most glaringly, Assurance misrepresented its financial statements and wrongly represented itself during the sale process as modestly profitable (and EBITDA positive), when in reality it was running at a significant loss .” Prudential says Assurance reported first-half 2019 health insurance revenue of $45.2 million and EBITDA of $728,691. Prudential also argues the insurers completed underwriting within weeks before the acquisition, reviewed the same diligence materials as the buyer, collected more than $6 million in premiums and fees, and have still not determined whether the claim is covered.

item.title: Leavitt Group of Colorado affiliates with Strategic Insurance
item.description: Leavitt Group of Colorado has affiliated with Strategic Insurance, expanding its presence in Colorado and increasing insurance carrier options and resources for clients while maintaining Strategic Insurance’s existing operations. Founded in 1952, Leavitt Group is one of the largest privately held insurance brokerages in the U.S., with more than 280 locations across 28 states offering commercial insurance, employee benefits, and personal insurance through locally owned agencies. Strategic Insurance, founded by Justin Burns, serves individuals, families, and businesses.

item.title: Zurich launches Life Sciences insurance solution across Europe
item.description: Zurich has launched a dedicated insurance solution for Life Sciences companies in Italy and eight other European markets, expanding expertise developed in the U.S. Coverage includes product liability, property, business interruption, marine, and accident and health insurance, with risk advisory services provided through Zurich Resilience Solutions. “Italy is a key market for the Life Sciences sector, characterized by highly specialized supply chains and a strong international presence.

item.title: Marsh appoints Teresa Palandra Canada CEO
item.description: Marsh has appointed Teresa Palandra as CEO of Canada, effective August 1, 2026. Based in Toronto, Palandra will report to Pat Tomlinson, President and CEO of Mercer and CEO of Marsh US and Canada. “A seasoned and innovative leader, Teresa will lead the strategic direction for Marsh Canada, ensuring we continue to strengthen our risk, people and investments capabilities and solutions for clients,” said Tomlinson.

item.title: Manulife Pursues AI-Powered Insurance Operating Model
item.description: But the core capability doesn’t change regardless of the audience or the segment.” That approach reflects the operating-model shift at the center of Manulife’s strategy: common enterprise capabilities, adapted by teams close to the business. Source: Manulife.) Manulife is seeking to move AI beyond individual use cases and productivity tools, treating the technology as a new operating capability for a global insurer. “When we talk about how Manulife is operating differently than maybe we have in the past, the clearest sign of that for us happened late last year when we elevated AI as a pillar of our enterprise strategy,” Havens says.

item.title: More room for softening after mid-year; Hub IPO could come in hot
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: MiCA filtro el mercado cripto, Krealo ficha banco IA pyme
item.description: Cocos integra Banco Voii y acelera su evolución hacia banco digital La fintech argentina Cocos completó la adquisición de Banco Voii , consolidando así el paso hacia un modelo de banca digital con licencia propia. Redeban lleva los pagos inmediatos al segmento empresarial El procesador de pagos colombiano lanzó Dispersiones, una funcionalidad que permite a las empresas realizar pagos masivos inmediatos a personas y compañías mediante llaves Bre-B o transferencias directas a cuentas bancarias. La solución habilita casos de uso como nómina, pagos a proveedores, subsidios y comisiones, reduciendo los tiempos de procesamiento a segundos.

item.title: Casualty sidecars claim 10% of market capacity as long-tail assets boost investor yields: S&P
item.description: A new report from S&P Global Ratings highlights a structural shift in the sidecar market, as casualty reinsurance sidecars have grown to account for approximately 10% of total sidecar capacity. Unlike traditional property sidecars that require high short-term liquidity, casualty sidecars leverage the long-tail nature of their underlying liabilities. “These vehicles’ evolving structures, together with supportive market conditions, have increased investor appetite for non-property catastrophe risks such as casualty re/insurance.

item.title: Clear Group strengthens leadership team with two senior executive appointments
item.description: Clear Group, an independent insurance and employee benefits group with operations across the UK and Ireland, has announced two senior executive appointments as the company continues to expand its leadership team in support of its long-term growth plans. I’m really looking forward to working with the team and helping grow the UK Retail business.” Mike Edgeley, Group CEO of Clear Group, added: “As Clear continues to evolve, it’s important that our leadership structure evolves alongside it. Clear Group said the appointments form part of its wider strategy as it continues to develop as a diversified insurance group operating across UK Retail, Brokerbility, Ireland, MGA and London Markets.

item.title: FCDO commits additional £3m to Natural Disaster Fund Technical Assistance Facility
item.description: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO), which leads the UK’s diplomatic, development and consular work globally, has committed an additional £3 million to the Technical Assistance Facility (NDF TA Facility), operated by the Natural Disaster Fund (NDF), a blended risk transfer vehicle designed to mitigate climate-related challenges and build resilience for low-and-middle-income countries. The move reflects the FCDO’s long-term commitment to enhancing climate resilience in the Global South, and aims to achieve wider engagement in the Climate and Disaster Risk Finance and Insurance (CDRFI) landscape. This will help develop insurance solutions that work for people in climate-vulnerable countries – driving more confident investment, resilient financial systems, and stronger, more stable growth.” Angus Kirk, Chief Executive Officer, Global Parametrics, commented, “This additional funding from the FCDO is a tribute to the continued successes of the NDF.

item.title: Competitive pressures and AI driving insurers to step up automation in underwriting: Sollers
item.description: Driven by intensifying competitive pressures, a softening market and rapid advances in artificial intelligence, four in 10 insurers are now using AI in underwriting, a move that marks a pivotal phase in their digitalisation, according to new research by Sollers Consulting. The automation of underwriting is rapidly becoming a strategic priority for insurers across all major markets. According to a Sollers study spanning ten markets, 126 AI use cases are currently active within the insurance sector, with 13 specifically focused on underwriting.

item.title: Baubap proyecta duplicar rentabilidad en su siguiente etapa de crecimiento
item.description: Nunca hemos habíamos optimizado por rentabilidad, sino por crecimiento. Sin embargo, hoy -por el volumen de negocio- necesitamos más robustez y estamos empujando a que nuestro margen de rentabilidad este año se duplique y sea alrededor del 15%”, dijo a iupana Roberto Salcedo, CEO de Baubap. Con más de 2 millones de usuarios en su cartera, la fintech planea llevar su operativa hacia la construcción de “la siguiente etapa evolutiva del negocio” donde tienen proyectado ampliar la oferta de valor y lanzar nuevos productos.

item.title: Austbrokers Countrywide, Adroit Insurance & Risk, Oneglobal, QBE, AIG: 14 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ILS funds report positive gains for May, with average return of 0.43%: ILS Advisers
item.description: The 0.43% average return for May 2026 is based on only 78% of ILS funds having reported their performance for the month so far, meaning with all ILS fund strategies positive for the month again, there is a strong chance the total rises. Insurance-linked securities investment fund strategies, across catastrophe bonds and private ILS, delivered another positive month of performance in May 2026, averaging a return of 0.43%, according to the ILS Advisers Fund Index For the second month in a row, all of the tracked ILS fund strategies that have reported performance figures so far were positive for the month. Last month, ILS funds had averaged a 0.53% return for April when ILS Advisers reported the data, but once all strategies had submitted data that rose to 0.58%.

item.title: Munich Re appoints Benhalima as Manager Structuring within Global ILS Solutions team
item.description: Munich Re, the German headquartered global reinsurance giant, has appointed property treaty underwriting specialist Lisa Benhalima as a Manager Structuring within its Global Insurance Linked Securities Solutions team. Now, in a LinkedIn communication, Benhalima has revealed her move into the insurance-linked securities business at Munich Re, becoming a Manager Structuring in the Global ILS Solutions team this month. From structuring catastrophe bonds and marketing them to investors, to designing bespoke risk transfer solutions that enable clients to tap into sources of institutional capital, Munich Re has always had dedicated specialists that can assist its own capital markets retrocessional needs (when it has them) and support clients in accessing sources of ILS capacity.

item.title: Yardstick Re flood cat bond underpins BarmeniaGothaer’s resilience to extreme events: CFO
item.description: For German insurance group BarmeniaGothaer the recent completion of its debut catastrophe bond sponsorship underpins its resilience to extreme events, while also offering useful single peril flood reinsurance coverage, CFO Harald Epple has said about the €100 million Yardstick Re DAC (Series 2026-1) issuance. In addition, access to the capital market provides us with additional capacity, offers us an alternative to traditional reinsurance markets and underpins our resilience to extreme events.” The company noted that the Yardstick Re catastrophe bond “fits seamlessly into the existing traditional reinsurance coverage and thus fulfills all the objectives associated with this risk transfer.” Having priced more than a week ago, as we reported at the time , the cat bond issuance has now settled and the Yardstick Re notes will provide BarmeniaGothaer’s insurer with €100 million of reinsurance limit to cover flood events in Germany over a four year term on an indemnity trigger and per-occurrence basis.

item.title: Reinsurers expanded options, embraced flexible structures at US property mid-year renewals: Aon
item.description: Global professional services firm Aon says reinsurers were increasingly willing to offer more flexible structures and broader coverage options during the US property mid-year 2026 renewals, allowing insurers to enhance protection and make greater use of tailored reinsurance solutions as market conditions remained favourable for buyers. In its Reinsurance Market Dynamics report covering the mid-year 2026 renewals, Aon said reinsurers demonstrated a broad appetite for expanded coverages and more flexible programme structures. Aon said global demand for property catastrophe reinsurance increased by more than 10% at the renewals, driven by higher catastrophe limits, broader product purchases and a significant increase in demand from Florida-based insurers.

item.title: RGA appoints Maurice Tulloch to its Board of Directors
item.description: Reinsurance Group of America, Incorporated (RGA), a global life and health reinsurer, has announced the appointment of Maurice Tulloch to its Board of Directors, effective 1st July 2026. “His combination of operational expertise and global insight, and his commitment to making insurance simpler and more responsive for customers will be valuable as RGA continues helping clients navigate change and deliver meaningful protection.” Stephen O’Hearn, Chair of the Board of Directors, RGA, added, “Maurice’s experience spans senior executive leadership, international insurance operations, and service on public and private boards. Tulloch currently serves on the boards of PSP Investments and Porch Group, Inc., and previously held board and industry leadership roles with Aviva plc, The Geneva Association, the Insurance Development Forum, ClimateWise, and the Insurance Bureau of Canada, among others.

item.title: Sidecar formation continued to increase in 2025 with total reserves exceeding $90bn: AM Best
item.description: The formation of sidecars experienced a significant increase in 2025, driven by strong annuity sales in the United States, according to a new report by credit rating agency AM Best. The Best’s Special Report, “Big Year of Growth for Life/Annuity Sidecar-Like Activity in 2025,” notes that the vast majority of reserves ceded are covering liabilities for indexed and fixed annuities, which have experienced significant growth amid rising interest rates over the last few years. During this period, total reserves ceded to these entities rose to over $90 billion, a sharp increase from the $55 billion recorded in 2023.

item.title: The Race to Rescue 8,000 Sailors Still Stranded Behind Hormuz
item.description: There were no signs of celebration from nearby vessels, and no ships making haste towards the Strait of Hormuz. “When they said the Strait of Hormuz was open, we were a bit optimistic that the vessel might transit,” he said. Around 8,000 seafarers who are not from the region remain stranded in the gulf, according to the International Maritime Organization, the United Nations agency that’s tasked with regulating and coordinating global shipping.

item.title: Coalition and Allianz Partner to Expand Active Cyber Insurance for Global Enterprises
item.description: The post Coalition and Allianz Partner to Expand Active Cyber Insurance for Global Enterprises appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Coalition has entered into a long-term strategic partnership with Allianz to expand its Active Cyber Insurance model into the global enterprise market, marking one of the most significant collaborations in the commercial cyber insurance sector. Combining Global Scale with Active Protection Coalition said the agreement combines its Active Cyber Insurance model which integrates continuous risk monitoring and proactive loss prevention with Allianz’s international distribution network and financial strength.

item.title: INSTANDA unveils AI-focused brand identity as it doubles down on intelligent insurance platform strategy
item.description: The post INSTANDA unveils AI-focused brand identity as it doubles down on intelligent insurance platform strategy appeared first on Insurtech Insights . INSTANDA, the London-based insurance technology provider, has introduced a refreshed brand identity that places artificial intelligence at the centre of its positioning, signalling a deeper shift in how it sees its role in modern insurance infrastructure. INSTANDA’s core offering remains its single-codebase, no-code policy administration platform, which allows insurers to design, launch, and manage insurance products without major system overhauls.

item.title: Income Insurance transfers HIVE platform to Embed Financial Group Holdings
item.description: Income is transferring its digital insurance platform, HIVE, to Singapore-based Embed Financial Group Holdings (EFGH), with the transaction expected to close in the third quarter of 2026. Developed by Income Insurance over the past five years, HIVE is an API-first platform that enables insurers and partners to embed insurance into digital customer journeys, supporting products such as microinsurance, subscription-based, and usage-based coverage. Employees responsible for developing and operating the platform will also transfer to EFGH, while Income Insurance will continue using HIVE as a customer.

item.title: Week in Review — June 29–July 2, 2026
item.description: And the June 2026 Monthly Innovation Report is live: 45 funding rounds totaling $16.96 billion, 30 strategic investments by 25 insurer-investors, 45 insurance acquisitions, and the latest ChatGPT insurance integrations. The same week, Sixth Street agreed to acquire a majority stake in Monument Re, the pan-European consolidator of in-force life portfolios, with Hannover Re staying on as a key shareholder. It was the second-largest insurance-related acquisition announced in June, behind the $372 million Open Lending take-private.

item.title: INSTANDA unveils AI-focused brand identity
item.description: INSTANDA has introduced a new AI-focused brand identity, highlighting its investment in artificial intelligence and expanding portfolio of AI capabilities. “Today, our brand evolves to reflect that. Hidden within the INSTANDA logo, two letters have been present from the very beginning: ‘A’ and ‘I’.

item.title: Fallece Juan Escrig Zamora, presidente de Mutua Levante
item.description: Mutua Levante acaba de comunicar o el fallecimiento de su presidente, Juan Escrig Zamora, quien ha estado al frente de la aseguadora y de Fundación Mutua Levante desde 2016 hasta el día de hoy.

item.title: El sector de las grúas plantea una tarifa específica para vehículos eléctricos
item.description: El objetivo era intercambiar información sobre la evolución del sector y analizar medidas relacionadas con la calidad y la sostenibilidad del servicio de auxilio en carretera. La Alianza Nacional de Auxilio en Carretera ha concluido una ronda de reuniones con compañías de asistencia en viaje, aseguradoras, automóviles clubes y plataformas que operan en España.

item.title: GerBrokers distribuirá seguros de Salud exclusivos para personas con discapacidad
item.description: Gracias a sendos acuerdos de colaboración, por un lado con Asisa y por otro con Aura Seguros, en ambos casos con el Consejo Español para la Defensa de la Discapacidad y la Dependencia (CEDDD), la correduría GerBrokers distribuirá un seguro Salud exclusivo para personas con discapacidad.

item.title: Lockton taps Alberto Ferriolo as Asia regional growth leader for marine, transportation
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles
